items.title: fix(java): transpiler callDynamically cast + Exchange.java stubs
items.body:
## Summary
- **Transpiler fix** (`build/javaTranspiler.ts`): Add `CompletableFuture` cast for `callDynamically().join()` calls across all 5 transpilation paths — fixes 44 compilation errors. Also fix test file output extension `.cs` → `.java` and test file naming.
- **Exchange.java stubs**: Add `binaryToBase64`, `exceptionMessage`, `ethGetAddressFromPrivateKey` to the hand-written section (needed by Aftermath, Grvt exchanges). Re-transpiled base section with the cast fix.

## Test plan
- [x] Clean build passes (0 errors, 0 failures)
- [x] Base tests pass (crypto, datetime, precise, etc.)
- [x] Live tests: Binance, OKX, Kraken, Bybit, Coinbase Exchange

items.title: Upgrade to Java 21
items.body:
Upgraded from java 11 to java 21
Upgraded junit from v5 to v6

This is a minimal PR. I would recommend some follow-up PRs:
- some code modernization/scrubbing taking advantage of newer java features
- `mvn compile` spits out some warnings which already existed before but should probably be cleaned up

Note: it seems that no additional code changes are needed to bump java from 21 to 25, so this could be considered.

----

- [x ] I read and understood <https://github.com/jgrapht/jgrapht/wiki/Become-a-Contributor>

Provide a clear description of how this change was tested.
At minimum this should include proof that a computer has executed the changed lines.
Ideally this should include an automated test or an explanation as to why this change has no tests.
Note that automated test coverage is less than complete, so a successful PR build does not necessarily imply that a computer has executed the changed lines.
If automated test coverage does not exist for the lines you are changing, you must describe the scenario(s) in which you manually tested the change.
For frontend changes, include screenshots of the relevant page(s) before and after the change.

items.title: Upgrade from Java 11 to Java 17
items.body: ## Summary Upgrades the project from Java 11 to Java 17 across the build toolchain, runtime images, and dependencies: **pom.xml:** - Java version: `11` → `17` - Spring Boot: `2.3.3.RELEASE` → `2.7.18` - Spring Cloud: `Hoxton.SR8` → `2021.0.8` - Cassandra Java Driver: `4.9.0` → `4.15.0` **Dockerfile (root):** All three stages updated from `openjdk-11`/`adoptopenjdk:11` to `eclipse-temurin:17` images. **docker/Dockerfile:** Both stages updated from `openjdk:11`/`adoptopenjdk:11` to `eclipse-temurin:17-jre`. Added `apt-get install wget` in the builder stage since `eclipse-temurin:17-jre` does not include `wget` (unlike the previous `openjdk:11-jre`), which is needed for the dockerize download. `mvn clean package`, `mvn test` (2/2 pass), and `docker build .` (root Dockerfile) all succeed locally. ## Review & Testing Checklist for Human - [ ] **Springfox 3.0.0 + Spring Boot 2.7.x runtime compatibility**: Springfox 3.0.0 is [known to break](https://github.com/springfox/springfox/issues/3462) with Spring Boot 2.6+ due to the default path matching strategy change. The app compiles but may fail at startup with a `NullPointerException`. Verify the app starts successfully and Swagger UI loads. If it doesn't, either add `spring.mvc.pathmatch.matching-strategy=ant_path_matcher` to `application.yml` or migrate to `springdoc-openapi`. - [ ] **Full application startup test**: Run the app against a real (or local Docker) Cassandra instance and exercise the REST API to catch any Java 17 strong encapsulation issues (illegal reflective access). If errors occur, `--add-opens` JVM flags may be needed. - [ ] **Cassandra driver 4.15.0 compatibility**: Verify the application connects to Astra DB / Cassandra correctly with the upgraded driver. The `cassandra-native-protocol.version` was left at `1.4.11` — confirm this pairing is valid. - [ ] **docker/Dockerfile build**: The `docker build .` was only verified for the root Dockerfile. The `docker/Dockerfile` requires build args (`ARTIFACT_NAME`, `DOCKERIZE_VERSION`) and a pre-built JAR, so it was not tested directly. Verify the `wget` install step works correctly in the builder stage. - [ ] **Spring Cloud Sleuth + Zipkin**: Verify distributed tracing still works end-to-end with Spring Cloud 2021.0.x. Sleuth should be compatible but worth a smoke test. ### Notes - `springfox.version` was intentionally left at `3.0.0` per the original request (verify-and-fix-if-broken approach), but this is the highest-risk item in the PR. - Only cosmetic Docker warnings remain (uppercase stage names, deprecated `MAINTAINER`), all pre-existing. - Test coverage is minimal (2 unit tests) — runtime verification is essential. Link to Devin session: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/f9926a5b71d74cc0bf29f21859eb4137 Requested by: @alok-desai ---

items.title: Appmod/java upgrade 20260411090728
items.body:
GUIFX:                                                                                                                                                                                                                    
  - Lock FFT size and bytes per sample at capture start to prevent
    buffer misalignment when user changes UI mid-capture                                                                                                                                                                    
  - Pre-allocate audio buffers once instead of per-frame allocation                                                                                                                                                         
  - Replace volatile boolean with AtomicBoolean for clean shutdown                                                                                                                                                          
  - Use ExecutorService for thread lifecycle management                                                                                                                                                                     
  - Add proper big/little-endian sample decoding                                                                                                                                                                            
  - Extract constants for magic numbers (threshold, smoothing, floor)                                                                                                                                                       
  - Handle partial reads from TargetDataLine                                                                                                                                                                                
                                                                                                                                                                                                                            
  XYLineChart:                                                                                                                                                                                                              
  - Fix frequency calculation (was double-nyquist, squashing graph)                                                                                                                                                         
  - Add gridlines and custom formatters (1k Hz, -20 dB)                                                                                                                                                                     
  - Auto-range Y axis for normalized dB display                                                                                                                                                                             
  - Apply CSS styling for visibility                                                                                                                                                                                        
                                                                                                                                                                                                                            
  pom.xml:                                                                                                                                                                                                                  
  - Add --enable-native-access flag to suppress JavaFX warnings                                                                                                                                                             
  - Add platform auto-detection profiles for cross-platform builds

items.title: Upgrade from Java 11 to Java 17
items.body: ## Summary Upgrades the project from Java 11 to Java 17, including all dependent framework and Docker image updates: - **pom.xml**: Java 11→17, Spring Boot 2.3.3→2.7.18, Spring Cloud Hoxton.SR8→2021.0.9 - **Springfox → springdoc-openapi**: Replaced `springfox-boot-starter` 3.0.0 (incompatible with Spring Boot 2.6+) with `springdoc-openapi-webflux-ui` 1.7.0. Rewrote both `ApiDocSecurityEnabledConfig` and `ApiDocSecurityDisabledConfig` to use springdoc's `GroupedOpenApi`/`OpenAPI` beans. Migrated all 6 controllers from Swagger 2 annotations (`@Api`, `@ApiOperation`, `@ApiResponse(code=, message=)`) to OpenAPI 3 annotations (`@Tag`, `@Operation`, `@ApiResponse(responseCode=, description=)`). - **SecurityEnabledConfig**: Updated permitted paths from `/v2/api-docs`, `/swagger-resources/**` to `/v3/api-docs/**` - **RestControllersConfig**: Updated home redirect from `/swagger-ui/` to `/swagger-ui.html` - **Dockerfiles**: Replaced `maven:3.6.3-openjdk-11`, `adoptopenjdk:11-jre-hotspot`, and `openjdk:11-jre` with `maven:3.9-eclipse-temurin-17` and `eclipse-temurin:17-jre` `mvn clean package` and `mvn test` (2 tests) pass locally with Java 17. ## Review & Testing Checklist for Human - [ ] **`docker/Dockerfile` builder stage**: The first stage changed from `openjdk:11-jre` (Debian-based, has `wget`) to `eclipse-temurin:17-jre` which may **not** have `wget` pre-installed. The `RUN wget ...` command on line 9 could fail at Docker build time. May need to add `RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y wget` or switch to `curl`. - [ ] **Runtime verification of Swagger UI**: Start the app and confirm `/swagger-ui.html` loads correctly and the OpenAPI spec is served at `/v3/api-docs`. The redirect from `/` should land on the Swagger UI page. - [ ] **Security-enabled mode**: When `petclinic.security.enable=true`, verify that `/v3/api-docs/**` and `/swagger-ui/**` are accessible without auth, and that the "Authorize" button appears in Swagger UI with the HTTP Basic scheme. - [ ] **Docker build**: Run `docker-compose build` to verify all Dockerfile stages succeed end-to-end with the new base images. - [ ] **Cassandra driver / jkube compatibility**: Build passes but these dependencies (4.9.0 / 1.0.2) were not individually verified against Java 17. Watch for reflection warnings at runtime due to Java 17's stronger encapsulation — may need `--add-opens` JVM flags. ### Notes - Only 2 unit tests exist in the repo; test coverage is minimal. The compilation + test pass provides limited confidence. - Spring Boot 2.7.x (not 3.x) was chosen intentionally to avoid the `javax` → `jakarta` namespace migration. - `@Parameter` annotations were already using `io.swagger.v3.oas.annotations.Parameter` in the original code, so they required no changes. Link to Devin session: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/f4a8e55a3a534e35b994c7786d1ed83f Requested by: @alok-desai ---

items.title: fix: Java cookbook recipes to compile with copilot-sdk-java 0.2.1-java.1
items.body:
## Summary

Fix compilation errors and documentation inaccuracies in Java cookbook recipes against the `copilot-sdk-java:0.2.1-java.1` API.

## Issues Found & Fixed

### Compilation failures (recipe/*.java)

| Recipe | Issue | Fix |
|--------|-------|-----|
| `MultipleSessions.java` | `CopilotSession.destroy()` does not exist | Replaced with `close()` |
| `AccessibilityReport.java` | `McpServerConfig` class does not exist | Replaced with `Map.of()` (matches `setMcpServers(Map<String, Object>)` signature) |

### Documentation inaccuracies (*.md inline code)

| File | Issue | Fix |
|------|-------|-----|
| `multiple-sessions.md` | `destroy()` in 2 code snippets | Replaced with `close()` |
| `accessibility-report.md` | `McpServerConfig` in 2 code snippets | Replaced with `Map.of()` |
| `error-handling.md` | `session.addTool()`, `ToolDefinition.builder()`, `ToolResultObject.success/error` — none exist | Replaced with `ToolDefinition.create()`, `SessionConfig.setTools()`, `CompletableFuture.completedFuture()` |

## Verification

All 7 recipes compile successfully with `jbang build`:

```
✅ ErrorHandling.java OK
✅ MultipleSessions.java OK
✅ ManagingLocalFiles.java OK
✅ PersistingSessions.java OK
✅ PRVisualization.java OK
✅ RalphLoop.java OK
✅ AccessibilityReport.java OK
```

## No changes needed

These recipes were already correct:
- `ErrorHandling.java` / `error-handling.md` (runnable file was fine; only docs section fixed)
- `ManagingLocalFiles.java` / `managing-local-files.md`
- `PersistingSessions.java` / `persisting-sessions.md`
- `PRVisualization.java` / `pr-visualization.md`
- `RalphLoop.java` / `ralph-loop.md`

items.title: ci: trigger sdk-platform-java CI on google-auth-library-java changes
items.body: This PR updates the path filter in `sdk-platform-java-ci.yaml` to include `google-auth-library-java/**`. This ensures that any changes to the auth library will also trigger the CI build for the SDK platform.
